verbGlossary.AUTHORIZES: {source} has final sign-off authority, granting {target} that allows work or payments to proceed.
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHES: {source}defines and sets up {target} pattern or approval chain for the project.
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source}requires {target} pattern as a contractual or regulatory condition on the project.
verbGlossary.CONFIGURES: {source}sets the parameters, steps, and routing rules for {target} component.
verbGlossary.AUDITS AGAINST: {source} performance against {target} metric for compliance or governance purposes.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS: {source} designates responsibility for an action item to a party vi{target}.
verbGlossary.ADMINISTERS: The organization manages and enforces {target} pattern or process on behalf of the project.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source}initiates this escalation or {target} event when conditions such as missed deadlines are met.
verbGlossary.CONTROLS: {source} has authority over this financial {target} stage, gating cost commitments or disbursements.
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {source}tracks {target} pattern or metric without direct approval authority.
verbGlossary.TRACKS: The organization uses {target} measure to monitor ongoing project performance over time.
verbGlossary.COORDINATES: The organization orchestrates this parallel or multi-party {target}, routing items to multiple reviewers simultaneously.
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source}creates the first record in draft status, starting {target} lifecycle.
verbGlossary.REVIEWS AT: {source} performs technical or compliance review of submissions at {target}.
verbGlossary.STAMPS: {source} applies its professional seal or formal endorsement at {target}, certifying technical adequacy.
verbGlossary.RETURNS TO: {source}sends a submission back to this revision {target}, requiring corrections before re-review.
verbGlossary.ISSUES: {source} formally issues a rejection or deficiency notice at {target}.
verbGlossary.SUBMITS AT: {source} formally delivers its work product or documentation at this submission {target}.
verbGlossary.HOLDS: The organization currently owns the action item in {target} and is responsible for advancing it.
verbGlossary.FALLS TO: {source}have missed their deadlines and transitioned to this overdue {target}.
verbGlossary.STAGES AT: The organization prepares completed work at this ready-for-review {target} before formal inspection begins.
verbGlossary.DELAYS AT: {source}delivery issues cause items to stall at this on-hold {target}, blocking downstream work.
verbGlossary.COMMITTED TO: {source} is contractually bound to deliver by this due date {target} component.
verbGlossary.PRODUCES DURING: {source} fabricates or manufactures components while the item is in this in-progress {target}.
verbGlossary.DELIVERS AT: The organization ships or hands off completed materials when the item reaches this submission {target}.
verbGlossary.PERFORMS: {source} carries out the inspection or testing activity defined by {target} pattern.
verbGlossary.CERTIFIES AS: {source} formally attests that the inspected item meets requirements, marking it at this conforming {target}.
verbGlossary.FLAGS AS: {source}identifies a deficiency or non-conformance, marking the item at this deficient {target}.
verbGlossary.EXECUTES: {source} carries out the full create-and-close {target} cycle for commissioning or testing activities.
verbGlossary.ADVANCES THROUGH: The organization progresses work through individual steps in {target} sequence.
verbGlossary.GRANTS: {source} issues formal {target} or permit at this status, allowing construction to proceed.
verbGlossary.ORDERS: The regulatory authority directs a mandatory stop or hold at {target}, halting work until conditions are resolved.
verbGlossary.CLEARS AT: The regulatory authority lifts a hold or violation, allowing the item to reach this closed {target}.
verbGlossary.DISBURSES AT: {source}releases funds at this revenue or billing stage based on verified progress.
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKS AGAINST: {source} performance against {target} metric as a risk or underwriting indicator.
verbGlossary.SIZED BY: {source}'s value is determined by the count or composition reflected in {target} component.
